name: Jamf Pro
description: Apple endpoint management platform for macOS, iOS and iPadOS. Yields smart group compliance reports, configuration profile deployment status, FileVault recovery key escrow evidence and device inventory via advanced computer searches and the Jamf Pro API.

steps.action: Sign in to Jamf Pro and navigate to Computers > Smart Computer Groups; open each compliance smart group (e.g. 'FileVault Not Enabled', 'OS Out of Date') and review the membership count and list
steps.permissions_required: Jamf Pro account with read privileges for Smart Computer Groups
steps.security_note: Use a dedicated read-only Jamf Pro account for evidence collection, not a full administrator login

steps.action: Navigate to Computers > Search Inventory, run the saved advanced computer search for your compliance criteria (encryption state, OS version, last check-in) and select Export to download the results as CSV
steps.permissions_required: Jamf Pro account with read privileges for Advanced Computer Searches
steps.audit_note: Export results reflect each device's last inventory submission — include the Last Inventory Update column so evidence freshness is verifiable

steps.action: Navigate to Computers > Configuration Profiles, open each security-relevant profile and record the deployment status (Completed/Pending/Failed counts) via screenshot or export
steps.audit_note: Capture failed installations as well — auditors expect exceptions to be visible and tracked, not filtered out

steps.action: Spot-check FileVault recovery key escrow: open a computer record and check Inventory > Disk Encryption shows a valid escrowed personal recovery key
steps.permissions_required: Jamf Pro account with the View Disk Encryption Recovery Key privilege (only if the key itself must be viewed)
steps.security_note: Evidence the escrow state, never the recovery key value — do not screenshot or export actual keys

steps.action: Upload the exports and screenshots to the evidence repository with date-stamped filenames and the evidence ID reference

L2.title: Jamf Pro Smart Group Notifications and Saved Searches
L2.estimated_time: 1 hour initial setup, then 10 minutes per cycle

steps.action: Configure SMTP server integration in Settings > System so Jamf Pro can send email notifications
On each compliance smart computer group, enable 'Send email notification on membership change' so drift out of compliance is reported automatically to the compliance mailbox
steps.audit_note: Notification emails are vendor-timestamped and demonstrate continuous monitoring of compliance drift between collection cycles

steps.action: Jamf Pro has no native scheduled report export — mitigate by saving an advanced computer search per evidence requirement so the weekly CSV export is a single click, or move to L3 API automation
Review computer inventory collection settings (Settings > Computer Management) to confirm inventory update frequency supports your evidence cadence
steps.audit_note: Evidence is only as fresh as the last inventory submission — a weekly report over stale inventory undermines evidentiary value

steps.action: File the notification emails and weekly search exports in the evidence repository each cycle
steps.security_note: Restrict the notification mailbox to authorised compliance personnel — membership changes reveal which devices are non-compliant

steps.action: In Jamf Pro, navigate to Settings > System > API Roles and Clients and create an API role with read-only privileges: Read Computers, Read Advanced Computer Searches and Read Smart Computer Groups
steps.permissions_required: Jamf Pro Administrator (to create API roles and clients)
steps.security_note: Grant the View Disk Encryption Recovery Key privilege only if key retrieval is genuinely required — escrow status can be evidenced without it

steps.action: Create an API client assigned to that role, enable it and generate a client secret
steps.security_note: The client secret is displayed only once — store it immediately in a secrets manager, never in plaintext config. Rotating the secret is required if the client's roles change.

steps.action: In the collection script, obtain an access token via POST /api/oauth/token with grant_type=client_credentials, client_id and client_secret
steps.security_note: Access tokens are short-lived — request a fresh token per run rather than caching long-lived credentials

steps.action: Call GET /api/v1/computers-inventory with the GENERAL, HARDWARE, OPERATING_SYSTEM and SECURITY sections (paginated) to export device inventory, encryption and compliance-relevant attributes daily
steps.permissions_required: API client with Read Computers privilege
steps.audit_note: Record the collection timestamp and total device count with each run so completeness can be reconciled

steps.action: For FileVault escrow evidence, call GET /api/v1/computers-inventory/filevault and record per-device escrow presence — discard the key material itself before writing evidence
steps.permissions_required: API client with Read Computers and View Disk Encryption Recovery Key privileges
steps.security_note: This endpoint returns personal recovery keys — process in memory, redact keys from stored evidence, and never log responses

steps.action: Schedule the script daily via cron or CI/CD, implement error handling and alerting for failed runs, and validate device counts against the previous run to detect silent gaps
steps.audit_note: Failed collections should trigger re-collection within SLA and be logged as exceptions

steps.action: In Jamf Pro, navigate to Settings > Global > Webhooks and create webhooks for ComputerInventoryCompleted and SmartGroupComputerMembershipChange pointing at your evidence pipeline or SIEM receiver
steps.security_note: Use an HTTPS endpoint with authentication enabled on the webhook — payloads identify devices and their compliance group membership

steps.action: Route webhook events into your SIEM (e.g. via the Jamf-documented Splunk integration) so inventory completions and compliance group changes are captured continuously
Jamf Pro webhooks are fire-and-forget with no delivery guarantee or replay — run a daily reconciliation against GET /api/v1/computers-inventory to detect and backfill missed events
steps.audit_note: The API reconciliation is what makes the webhook stream defensible as complete evidence

steps.action: Configure completeness and quality metrics: percentage of devices with inventory updated within the last 7 days, FileVault escrow coverage percentage, and smart group membership deltas per day
steps.audit_note: Coverage metrics against the full enrolment baseline demonstrate collection completeness, not just activity

steps.action: Build a dashboard showing pipeline health (events per day, reconciliation mismatches, stale-inventory devices) and alert when webhook volume drops to zero or reconciliation finds gaps
steps.audit_note: The dashboard serves as continuous evidence of a managed collection process

steps.action: Automate a quarterly collection-health report summarising missed events, backfills and SLA breaches, and file it in the evidence repository
